  The patch changes the requested OIDC audience to a value the current cyspbot verifier does not accept, breaking default token exchanges. It also exposes read permissions that the service rejects as unsupported scopes.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Keep the OIDC audience compatible with cyspbot — /Users/cysp/src/cyspbot-app-token-action/src/action.ts:57-59
  With the default `cyspbot-url`, every run now requests an ID token with audience `https://github.com/apps/cyspbot`, but cyspbot's current GitHub Actions OIDC verifier is configured to accept `aud` = `cyspbot`; those tokens fail verification before the token policy is evaluated. Unless the service is deployed with matching trusted audiences, the action will return an auth error for all default users.

- [P2] Reject unsupported read permission scopes — /Users/cysp/src/cyspbot-app-token-action/src/action.ts:207-211
  When a caller sets any `permission-*` input to `read`, this validation accepts it and sends scopes such as `contents:read`, but cyspbot currently only normalizes `actions:write`, `contents:write`, and `pull_requests:write`; the documented read mode therefore always fails with `invalid_scope`. Either keep the action limited to supported write scopes or update the service contract before advertising/forwarding read permissions.
  </results>
  </user_action>

name: Get cyspbot app token
description: Exchange a GitHub Actions OIDC token for a short-lived repository-scoped GitHub App installation token from cyspbot.

inputs:
  audience:
    description: Canonical GitHub App URL to request, such as https://github.com/apps/cyspbot.
    required: false
    default: https://github.com/apps/cyspbot
  cyspbot-url:
    description: Base URL for the cyspbot service.
    required: false
    default: https://cyspbot.chikachow.org
  resource:
    description: Canonical GitHub repository API URI to request, such as https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo. Omitted by default.
    required: false
  scope:
    description: Space-delimited GitHub App permission scopes to request, such as contents:write pull_requests:write. Omitted by default.
    required: false

outputs:
  token:
    description: Short-lived GitHub App installation token.
  expires_at:
    description: Expiration timestamp for the minted installation token.

runs:
  using: node24
  main: dist/index.js
```

Outputs:

- `token`
- `expires_at`

Inputs:

- `audience`
  - canonical GitHub App URL, such as `https://github.com/apps/cyspbot`
  - default: `https://github.com/apps/cyspbot`
- `cyspbot-url`
  - default: `https://cyspbot.chikachow.org`
- `resource`
  - optional canonical GitHub repository API URI, such as `https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo`
- `scope`
  - optional space-delimited GitHub App permission scopes, such as `contents:write pull_requests:write`

The action requests a GitHub Actions OIDC token whose audience is the `audience` input and sends the same value as the token-exchange `audience`. When `resource` or `scope` are omitted, cyspbot applies its service defaults for the verified workflow principal. Blank values are treated as omitted by the action. Non-blank values are validated locally, then sent to cyspbot for service-owned token policy validation.

Example use with `peter-evans/create-pull-request`:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write
  pull-requests: write

steps:
  - uses: chikachow/cyspbot-app-token-action@v0.0.3
    id: cyspbot
    with:
      scope: contents:write pull_requests:write

  - uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v8
    with:
      token: ${{ steps.cyspbot.outputs.token }}
```

## Documentation

- For repository structure and maintenance boundaries, see [`docs/REPOSITORY.md`](docs/REPOSITORY.md).
- For local development, release workflow, and engineering conventions, see [`docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`](docs/DEVELOPMENT.md).

- `action.yml` points at `dist/index.js`.
- `dist/index.js` is generated during release preparation and committed onto release-tagged commits, not onto `main`.
- The runtime artifact stays self-contained. Consumers do not install this repository's dependencies at action execution time.
- The action exposes `token` and `expires_at` outputs and accepts `audience`, `cyspbot-url`, `resource`, and `scope` inputs.
- Blank `audience` defaults to `https://github.com/apps/cyspbot`; the action uses it to request a GitHub Actions OIDC token and sends the same URL as the token-exchange `audience`. Blank `resource` and `scope` inputs are omitted from the token exchange request. Non-blank values are trimmed, validated locally, and forwarded to cyspbot for service-owned policy validation.
- `main` is the source branch, not a supported consumer ref for `uses:`.
- `vX.Y.Z` tags are GitHub Release tags created by the release workflows.
- `vX.Y` and `vX` tags are compatibility tags without corresponding GitHub Releases, so they move forward to the latest compatible stable release commit.
